behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: module handoff/wiring error in the aggregation path (one source omitted before summary generation). Required setup: wire collector modules plus aggregator and assert the payload sent to summary generation includes metrics endpoint, issue tracker API, and deployment log fields. Rejection-gate: System was considered but rejected because this behavior is about payload composition at a module boundary; real OS/network failures are not required to expose omission bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Run the data-collection and assembly modules together with deterministic test doubles for the three source clients; intercept the call into summary generation and assert all three source sections are present and mapped to the expected schema.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: cross-module error-handling/wiring error (source failure propagates incorrectly and aborts pipeline, or fallback note not inserted). Required setup: source client, aggregation/report builder, and summary input builder wired together with one source raising an error. Rejection-gate: System was considered but rejected because this behavior can be triggered by an in-process exception/timeout stub from one source client; real network outage is not necessary for the fallback logic failure.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the reporting pipeline modules with two sources returning data and one source client failing deterministically; assert the run completes, includes a `data unavailable` marker for that section, and still emits a report payload.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: AI judgment-quality error (hallucination, omission, or misstatement of key values). Required setup: real summary model call and evaluation rubric/checker over multiple runs/prompts. Rejection-gate: Workflow was considered but rejected because behavior text specifies a single summary-generation outcome, not multiple agentic stages in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Feed representative report inputs with known key values into the real summary-generation model, run repeated trials, and score outputs against factuality checks (key metrics/issues/deployments must be preserved without contradiction). Fail if accuracy drops below threshold.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: environment/external-system error in end-to-end delivery path (SMTP/API auth, transport, formatting, recipient routing). Required setup: run full script with real external interfaces and verify arrival in an actual mailbox/test inbox. Rejection-gate: Agentic was considered but rejected because this behavior text validates delivery to inbox and end-to-end transport; it does not require judging LLM output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run the script end-to-end against reachable source endpoints (or system-level test services) and a real mail delivery endpoint tied to a test inbox; assert message is received by configured recipient with expected subject/body/attachments and no pipeline abort.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: environment/external-system plus persistence/retry lifecycle error (unreachable email service handling, filesystem persistence across runs, retry on next schedule). Required setup: real filesystem state and two script executions with mail endpoint unavailable then available. Rejection-gate: Integration was considered but rejected because this behavior explicitly depends on local file durability and next scheduled run semantics, which commonly fail at real process/OS boundaries.
behaviors.test_description: Execute run 1 with email endpoint intentionally unreachable; assert report file is written to disk in retry queue location. Execute run 2 (simulating next schedule) with email restored; assert queued report is sent and retry artifact is cleared/marked delivered.
